summary: v2.17 (multi-project + strict seed + 3-level subtasks) — ALL GREEN end-to-end. Backend 13/13 pytest (test_v217.py, 4.29s): project CRUD + default Personal hydration + role/shared/member_count + cannot-delete-default; share endpoint with non-existent invitee email creates pending member; member role update + remove; comments post+list; project_id filter on tasks/routines/transactions/notes/reminders/deadlines (all 6 collections); 3-level subtask chain via parent_id round-trips for tasks/routines/transactions; reset/seed requires confirm:'RESET'; seed/first-login is idempotent (returns seeded:false when user already has data). Frontend (Playwright 1920): LOGIN✓; ProjectSelector top-right shows current project with color dot✓; clicking opens dropdown with 2 projects (Personal Default + TEST_v217_UIProj)✓; switching projects re-fetches data (Personal has 8 task rows, new project has 0)✓; '+ New project' button creates project via prompt and switches to it✓; ShareDialog opens via per-project share button — email input, role select, owner row with admin role all present✓; Tasks page renders 3-level subtask chain with data-depth='0'|'1'|'2' attribute + progressive indentation (L1 parent → L2 child → L3 grandchild visible in screenshot)✓; depth=2 row correctly has NO task-subtask-{id} button (capped at 2-level subtask creation, total 3 visible depths)✓; CashFlow split-bill creates child line items 3 levels deep with tx-subtask-row + pl-10/pl-20 indent, and depth=2 row also has no +subtask button✓.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line ~2426 _purge_legacy_demo_data — signatures dict matches OLD demo rows (Coffee Shop, Welcome reminder, Quarterly Review, Shopping List) and wipes you@mindmatters.local user's data. Idempotent + only runs on startup. Clean.

server.py line ~2450 _backfill_default_projects — iterates all users and calls _ensure_default_project; safe but O(N) on startup. For >5K users add an index on projects.owner_id and bulk-find users-without-projects via $lookup. Not urgent for current scale.

server.py line ~2887 seed_first_login — checks tasks+routines+transactions counts and bails if ANY non-empty. Correct idempotency. NOTE: re-seed only via /api/reset/seed with confirm='RESET'. Test user Karan already has data from prior iterations so strict-seed labels (Zomato/Brinda/Rahul/Amit) are NOT present in his Personal project — this is expected behavior, not a bug.

ProjectSelector.jsx — share button is per-project (data-testid='project-share-{id}') inside the dropdown list, not on the top-level selector button. Initial test missed this; final test confirmed both Personal and other projects expose share. Consider also wiring a top-level share affordance on the active project for discoverability.

ShareDialog.jsx data-testids are consistent: share-dialog, share-close, share-email-input, share-role-select, share-invite-btn, share-owner-row, share-member-{id}, share-member-role-{id}, share-member-remove-{id}. Excellent testability.

/lib/nestRows.js — generic helper now powers Tasks/Routines/CashFlow via a single reducer (3-level interleave with depthPaddingClass). Orphan-row fallback (parent missing/filtered) surfaces flat at depth 0 — documented behavior. DRY win over v2.16's 3 near-identical reducers.

Axios interceptor on /lib/api.js injects project_id on /tasks /routines /transactions /notes /reminders /deadlines — both query (GET) and body (POST). Confirmed live in test: switching project drops task list from 8→0 and back to 8.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all v2.17 features confirmed: ProjectSelector dropdown, project create+switch, ShareDialog with email/role/owner, 3-level subtask creation for Tasks (visible L1→L2→L3 in screenshot) + CashFlow, depth=2 cap on +subtask button, data-depth attribute present, project switch re-fetches data

seed_data_creation: Backend tests created and cleaned TEST_v217_* fixtures via finally blocks. Frontend test created persistent: TEST_v217_UIProj (new project), TEST_v217_d1_sub + TEST_v217_d2_sub (task subtasks in Personal), TEST_v217_tx_d1 + TEST_v217_tx_d2 (cashflow split-bill children). These can be cleaned manually if needed but do not interfere with future tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: v2.17 is fully green. Key selectors for next agent: ProjectSelector — data-testid='project-selector', 'project-selector-btn' (top dropdown trigger), 'project-dropdown', 'project-item-{id}' (list rows in dropdown), 'project-share-{id}' (per-project share btn inside dropdown), 'project-create-btn' (+ New project at bottom of dropdown). ShareDialog — 'share-dialog', 'share-close', 'share-email-input', 'share-role-select', 'share-invite-btn', 'share-owner-row', 'share-member-{id}', 'share-member-role-{id}', 'share-member-remove-{id}'. Row depth attribute is data-depth='0'|'1'|'2' on every task/routine/tx row. Subtask buttons are 'task-subtask-{id}' / 'routine-subtask-{id}' / 'tx-subtask-{id}' present ONLY on depth<2 rows. Karan's account already has lots of test data so /api/seed/first-login returns seeded:false — to verify strict seed labels live, signup as a brand-new user. Project filter: axios interceptor injects project_id on data routes when localStorage.mm_current_project is set; tests must either trust the interceptor or pass project_id explicitly (backend test_v217.py uses explicit project_id and works perfectly). Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_v217.py — 13 tests, 4.29s, all pass.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failures. The four initially-FAIL items (seed labels Zomato/Brinda/Rahul/Amit) were a false negative caused by Karan having pre-existing data from iterations 1–14, which correctly suppresses strict-seed via the idempotency guard. Backend test test_seed_first_login_is_idempotent_when_data_present confirms this is intended behavior.
